channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump will use drones if cartels don't withdraw
item.description: "If they don't recede, you're gonna see dead bodies all over the place and that's coming soon," O'Reilly says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk creates confusion about IRS' Direct File
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire tech mogul Elon Musk posted Monday on his social media site that he had “deleted” 18F, a government agency that worked on technology projects such as the IRS’ Direct File program.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Pam Bondi AG nomination
item.description: The Senate on Monday teed up a final vote on Pam Bondi's nomination to lead the Justice Department. The chamber voted 52-46 to limit debate. Absent an agreement with Democrats, that puts a final confirmation vote on the docket for the early hours of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump agriculture nominee, Brooke Rollins, clears key Senate hurdle ahead of confirmation vote
item.description: Brooke Rollins, the former president and CEO of the America First Policy Institute, had earned broad support from farmers and growers' groups ahead of her Senate confirmation vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk tightens grip on federal government as Democrats raise alarms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is rapidly consolidating control over large swaths of the federal government with President Donald Trump ’s blessing, sidelining career officials, gaining access to sensitive databases and dismantling a leading source of humanitarian assistance.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Omar slams Trump, Musk for changes at USAID, accuses president of running dictatorship
item.description: Democratic lawmakers stood on the steps of USAID headquarters in Washington, D.C. on Monday and called out President Trump and Elon Musk for attempting to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge in nation's capital extends block on Trump administration federal funding freeze
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge in the nation's capital kept a temporary block on a Trump administration plan for a freeze on federal funding Monday after some nonprofit groups said they're still struggling to get promised grants and loans. U.S. District Judge Loren L.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pauses Canadian tariffs for 30 days in deal with Trudeau
item.description: President Trump and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau agreed to a pause in implementing tariffs for at least a month, just hours after Trump agreed to delay tariffs on Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP's Susan Collins to back Tulsi Gabbard confirmation
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine) announced Monday that she will support Tulsi Gabbard to become director of national intelligence (DNI), giving her a key boost in her confirmation push.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ Dem gubernatorial candidates vow to impede ICE pursuit of illegal aliens
item.description: New Jersey Democratic gubernatorial candidates vowed to protect illegal immigrants from President Trump’s ICE raids during the Garden State’s first primary debate of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Republicans introduce bill to reform birthright citizenship, following Trump’s controversial order
item.description: Following Trump’s executive order to end birthright citizenship for the children of illegal immigrants, several Republicans introduced a bill that would reform U.S. law to accomplish exactly that.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal agencies scrub climate change from websites amid Trump rebranding
item.description: Federal agencies are beginning to remove references to climate change from their websites, as Trump issued a rebranding of departments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk-led DOGE effectively shuts down USAID
item.description: Democratic lawmakers say shutting down the U.S. Agency for International Development is illegal, vowing legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order to create US sovereign wealth fund, eyeing TikTok purchase
item.description: President Trump on Monday directed secretaries to begin a process to create an American sovereign wealth fund, suggesting it could partially own the popular app TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: At the regional level, provinces are taking their own actions, with multiple premiers saying they have directed their respective liquor boards to stop selling American alcohol.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says El Salvador’s president has offered to accept deportees from the U.S. of any nationality, including violent American criminals now imprisoned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Another week to be shaped by court fight over power dynamic within Minnesota Legislature
item.description: There’s another lawsuit headed to the Minnesota Supreme Court this week regarding the power standoff in the House that’s bleeding into week four. Meanwhile, the Senate returned to DFL control.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk says President Donald Trump has 'agreed' USAID should be shut down
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Agency for International Development is on the cusp of being shuttered, according the Trump administration's billionaire adviser and Tesla CEO Elon Musk — who has been wrestling for control of the agency in recent days.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump defends tariffs as Mexico, Canada and China plan retaliation
item.description: Some of the top imported products from the United States include cars, produce, alcohol, cellphones, clothes and shoes.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: As Trump vows to embrace fossil fuels, U.S. climate policy won't change quickly
item.description: President Trump's first weeks in office included executive orders that aim to stop or roll back former President Biden's ambitious climate agenda and redirect the country's attention to fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI attacks
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, Fox 5

item.title: Politics Friday: Democrats seek a new leader with Minnesota’s DFL party chair
item.description: MPR News politics editor and host Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the Democratic National Committee’s election of a new party chair and the Minnesotan who’s campaigning for the job. Later, a conversation with newly elected Minnesota Republican Party Chair Alex Plechash.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: New York expands efforts to stop human trafficking
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York is expanding its efforts to stop human trafficking. Gov. Kathy Hochul recently signed a series of bills to shed light on the issue, which targets young women. The NYPD's human trafficking task force conducted more than 300 operations in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy? You may recognize him from MTV
item.description: Duffy, formerly a reality TV star and Wisconsin congressman, was confirmed as the new transportation secretary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Fox 5

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5
